_: WKNDR TASTE CORPUS — Ness's judgments, injected into every judge (editor + vision). Grows with each curation round. v1 = Curation Board round 1, 2026-07-02, 86 verdicts.
imageRules: Prefer atmosphere WITH people in the real place over empty rooms or generic scenery — a market mid-shop, a festival crowd at golden hour, a terrace in use.
For an artist or performer: their actual work or a genuine photo of them — never a stand-in, never another venue.
For theatre/shows: a cast or performance photo beats the venue and beats the poster.
Editorial/official-quality photography preferred; anything that reads as generic stock is bad even when technically on-subject.
No odd crops — the subject must survive the tall portrait frame.
Designed posters with pictorial imagery are fine; text-only posters, logos and wordmarks are not.
Never a different identifiable person, never another venue's recognizable landmark.

imageExemplars.why: chose the festival crowd in the park over a generic shot
chose the floating market seen from the water — the thing that makes it singular

eventRules: Community-authentic gatherings are NOT filler: neighbourhood singing sessions, maker markets, museum markets, open-air food culture score WELL when they are genuinely local and warm.
Food culture is core WKNDR: supper clubs, chef one-offs, food-film mashups, brunch institutions rate high.
Design/craft retail with a strong point of view (concept stores, ateliers, specialist shops) rates high as weekend material.
Quirky-unique formats (a lying-down concert, cinema-dinner hybrids) are exactly the surprising texture the deck needs.

KILL on sight: tribute acts and cover shows; VR/game/experience museums; branded comedy-franchise nights; church lunch concerts; generic revue/cabaret listings; arthouse reruns with no occasion; wide-release cinema.
Institutional museum wallpaper (a permanent collection with no distinct show) ranks low; a distinct exhibition with strong imagery ranks high.
Recurring is fine when it is beloved and local (a weekly market, a legendary club night); recurring is filler when it is a venue's self-promo.

starAnchors.title: De Roode Remise Singing Session
starAnchors.note: the judge called this filler (1); Ness rates community-authentic warmth highly

starAnchors.title: Museum Market / The Maker Market
starAnchors.note: judge said filler; wrong — beloved local markets

starAnchors.title: Homelanding
starAnchors.note: hero event but a weak card — generic image dragged it

eventVeto: cabaret at the end of the world
_vetoNote: Taste kills from the board — dropped at publish, every run, any source. Dupe-kills (Julidans/TREK/Vondelpark twins) are deliberately NOT here: those were dedup actions; their kept twins scored ★3-4.
